content:
# Colossians Chapter 4 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-05

---

### v.3 — "a door for the word" vs. "many opportunities to speak about his mysterious plan"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of the Christos" | thyran tou logou |
| LOGOS | "that God may open to us a door for the word, to declare the mystery of Christ" | Same — preserves "door" metaphor |
| PNEUMA | "that God will give us many opportunities to speak about his mysterious plan concerning Christ" | "Many opportunities" — pluralizes; "mysterious plan" for mystērion |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul asks for "a door for the word" — singular, metaphorical. PNEUMA pluralizes to "many opportunities," loses the door metaphor, and changes "mystery of Christ" to "mysterious plan concerning Christ" — expanding and restructuring.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA loses door metaphor and pluralizes.

---

### v.5 — "redeeming the time" vs. "making the best use of the time" vs. "make the most of every opportunity"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "redeeming the time" | exagorazomenoi ton kairon |
| LOGOS | "making the best use of the time" | Same as Ephesians 5:16 |
| PNEUMA | "make the most of every opportunity" | "Every opportunity" — pluralizes kairos |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Same phrase as Ephesians 5:16. PNEUMA again pluralizes the singular kairos.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Consistent with Ephesians 5:16 decision.

---

### v.6 — "seasoned with salt" vs. "gracious and attractive"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "[Let] your word [be] always in grace, having been seasoned with salt" | halati ērtymenos |
| LOGOS | "Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "Let your conversation be gracious and attractive so that you will have the right response for everyone" | "Attractive" replaces "seasoned with salt"; adds "right response" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA replaces "seasoned with salt" — a vivid metaphor — with "attractive," losing the concrete image. The salt metaphor carries connotations of preservation, flavor, and wisdom that "attractive" does not.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA loses the salt metaphor.

---

### v.11 — "of the circumcision" vs. "Jewish believers"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the only ones of the circumcision [who are] fellow workers" | hoi ontes ek peritomēs |
| LOGOS | "the only men of the circumcision among my fellow workers" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "the only Jewish believers among my co-workers" | "Jewish believers" for "of the circumcision" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. "Of the circumcision" (ek peritomēs) is Paul's technical term for Jewish identity defined by covenant sign. PNEUMA renders as "Jewish believers" — correct in reference but losing the covenantal marker language that Paul uses deliberately.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Minor — PNEUMA loses covenantal term.

---

### v.12 — "stand complete and fully assured" vs. "stand mature and fully assured" vs. "make you strong and perfect, fully confident"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "that you may stand complete and fully assured in all [the] will of God" | teleios kai peplērophorēmenoi |
| LOGOS | "that you may stand mature and fully assured in all the will of God" | "Mature" for teleios |
| PNEUMA | "asking God to make you strong and perfect, fully confident that you are following the whole will of God" | "Strong and perfect" — doubles; "following" — adds |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA doubles teleios into "strong and perfect" (two words for one) and adds "following" — Paul says "in all the will of God," not "following the will of God." Standing in God's will is different from following it.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA doubles and adds "following."

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA pluralizes singular | 2 | "Many opportunities" (v.3), "every opportunity" (v.5) |
| PNEUMA loses metaphors | 2 | "Door for the word" (v.3), "seasoned with salt" (v.6) |
| PNEUMA doubles terms | 1 | "Strong and perfect" for teleios (v.12) |
| PNEUMA loses technical terms | 1 | "Of the circumcision" → "Jewish believers" (v.11) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 4's disagreements are relatively minor — the closing chapter is personal greetings with less theological density. The most notable pattern is PNEUMA's consistent loss of Paul's metaphors: the "door" for proclamation opportunity (v.3) and the "salt" for speech quality (v.6). Paul's concrete images carry more freight than their abstract replacements.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
